utilities.note: electric (AC) + water + home fibre internet; usage-sensitive in hot season

groceries.note: markets + some home cooking; food total cross-checks around $250-270/mo with dining

diningOut.note: street food daily + occasional restaurants; meals $1-7

simData.source: AIS / TrueMove H prepaid data plans
simData.note: Thai prepaid monthly data; local SIM cheaper than tourist eSIMs

misc.source: asiapicks worked budget; casabasilico example budget

accomSeasonality._doc: Rent-only multipliers. CM peak = cool/dry Nov-Feb; troughs = burning season Mar-Apr (low demand) and green/rainy Jun-Sep.

evidence.claim: Furnished 1BR Nimman with pool/gym ~12k THB ($335); nomad-ready range $350-600
evidence.quote: a sleek one-bedroom with a pool, gym, and 24-hour security costs 12,000 baht ($335) per month
newer serviced apartments in Nimman with facilities run 10,000 to 16,000 THB
coworking at Punspace costs $120 ... Yellow and Punspace typically charge around THB 3,000-4,000 / USD 80-100/month
food costs for market breakfasts, street food dinners, and occasional restaurant meals run approximately $250

evidence.claim: Worked mid-range example totals ~$900: studio $350, coworking $120, food $250, scooter $80, misc $100
evidence.quote: a private studio with AC near Nimman at $350, coworking membership at $120, food at $250, scooter rental at $80, and miscellaneous expenses at $100, totaling ~$900
Total monthly costs with housing come to just $869, while digital nomads spend around $1,257 per month on average

evidence.claim: Thai local prepaid mobile plans support a low monthly SIM line item versus tourist eSIM pricing
evidence.quote: prepaid mobile data plans are available from AIS and TrueMove H
